title: malva parviflora var. flexuosa
intro: Malva parviflora var. flexuosa, also known as the small mallow or dwarf mallow, is a subspecies of the Malva parviflora plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it beneficial for treating skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Malva parviflora var. flexuosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as a gentle laxative. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, can be used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
